creator: dpchrist@holgerdanske.com
text:
I have a computer:

    Intel DQ67SWR motherboard
    Intel Core i7-2600S processor
    8 GB RAM
    16 GB SSD


I downloaded:

CHECKSUM.SHA256-FreeBSD-10.3-RELEASE-i386
FreeBSD-10.3-RELEASE-i386-memstick.img.xz


I extracted the second and verified the checksum:

2016-09-25 13:23:53 dpchrist@cd2533 ~/Data/dpchrist/iso/freebsd/10.3
$ sha256sum -c CHECKSUM.SHA256-FreeBSD-10.3-RELEASE-i386 2> /dev/null | grep i386-memstick.img 
FreeBSD-10.3-RELEASE-i386-memstick.img: OK
FreeBSD-10.3-RELEASE-i386-memstick.img.xz: OK


I copied FreeBSD-10.3-RELEASE-i386-memstick.img to a USB flash drive:

2016-09-25 13:29:14 dpchrist@t7400 ~
$ lsusb -v -s 001:003

Bus 001 Device 003: ID 125f:c08a A-DATA Technology Co., Ltd. 
Couldn't open device, some information will be missing
Device Descriptor:
  bLength                18
  bDescriptorType         1
  bcdUSB               2.00
  bDeviceClass            0 (Defined at Interface level)
  bDeviceSubClass         0 
  bDeviceProtocol         0 
  bMaxPacketSize0        64
  idVendor           0x125f A-DATA Technology Co., Ltd.
  idProduct          0xc08a 
  bcdDevice            1.00
  iManufacturer           1 
  iProduct                2 
  iSerial                 3 
  bNumConfigurations      1
  Configuration Descriptor:
    bLength                 9
    bDescriptorType         2
    wTotalLength           32
    bNumInterfaces          1
    bConfigurationValue     1
    iConfiguration          0 
    bmAttributes         0x80
      (Bus Powered)
    MaxPower              480mA
    Interface Descriptor:
      bLength                 9
      bDescriptorType         4
      bInterfaceNumber        0
      bAlternateSetting       0
      bNumEndpoints           2
      bInterfaceClass         8 Mass Storage
      bInterfaceSubClass      6 SCSI
      bInterfaceProtocol     80 Bulk-Only
      iInterface              0 
      Endpoint Descriptor:
        bLength                 7
        bDescriptorType         5
        bEndpointAddress     0x01  EP 1 OUT
        bmAttributes            2
          Transfer Type            Bulk
          Synch Type               None
          Usage Type               Data
        wMaxPacketSize     0x0200  1x 512 bytes
        bInterval               1
      Endpoint Descriptor:
        bLength                 7
        bDescriptorType         5
        bEndpointAddress     0x82  EP 2 IN
        bmAttributes            2
          Transfer Type            Bulk
          Synch Type               None
          Usage Type               Data
        wMaxPacketSize     0x0200  1x 512 bytes
        bInterval               1


I attempted to use the USB flash drive to install FreeBSD:

    a.  Power down.

    b.  Insert FreeBSD-10.3-RELEASE-i386-memstick.img USB stick into
	rear USB 2 slot.  Insert Samsung 16 GB SSD into upper docking
	bay.
	
    c.  Power up.
    
    d.  Press F10 during post to bring up Boot Menu.  Use cursor keys
	to highlight USB device.  Press Enter to boot.  Ignore warning:

	    gptboot: backup GPT header checksum mismatch

    e.  At "FreeBSD - Welcome to FreeBSD" screen, cboose
    
	    1.  Boot Multi User [Enter]
	
	or wait for timeout.

    f.  At "FreeBSD Installer - Welcome" screen, choose:

	    Install

    g.  At "FreeBSD Installer - Keymap Selection" screen, choose:

	    Continue with default keymap

    h.  At "FreeBSD Installer - Set Hostname" screen, enter:

	    freebsd

    i.  At "FreeBSD Installer - Distribution Selection" screen, enable
	the following selections:

	    doc
	    games
	    ports
	    src

    j.  At "FreeBSD Installer - Partitioning" screen, choose:

	    Auto (ZFS)

    k.  At "FreeBSD Installer - ZFS Configuration" screen, configure as
	follows:

	    Pool Type/Disks		stripe: 1 disk
		ada0
	    ...
	    Pool Name			zroot
	    Force 4K Sectors		YES
	    Encrypt Disks		YES
	    Partition Scheme		MBR (BIOS)
	    Swap Size			2g
	    Mirror Swap			NO
	    Encrypt Swap		YES

	Then choose "Install".  Confirm destruction of contents of
	ada0.  Enter encryption passphrase.

    l.  Hangs on screen "FreeBSD Installer - Archive Extraction":

	    ports.txz		23%
